evals.prompt: The crawl returned a rule called canonical-mismatch and I have no idea what it means or whether I should care. Can you explain it?
evals.expected_output: Should use report id explain-crawl-issue to return a plain-English explanation of one crawler rule with fix and verification steps. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_describe_report with id explain-crawl-issue, then seo_run_report with the ruleId. Should read category, default severity, and why it matters first, then the fix steps and ignored impact, then the verification method and agent hints. Should describe the checked condition without claiming a search-engine penalty or a guaranteed ranking effect.
assertions: Uses report id explain-crawl-issue to explain the rule
Passes the ruleId for the rule in question
Reads category, default severity, and why it matters first
Reads fix steps and ignored impact, then verification and agent hints
Describes the checked condition without claiming a penalty or ranking effect

evals.prompt: Explain ruleId title-too-long and tell me how to verify the fix once we change the titles.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id explain-crawl-issue with ruleId title-too-long, via seo reports run explain-crawl-issue or seo_run_report. Should read category, default severity, and why it matters, then the fix steps and ignored impact, then the verification method. Should note that the rule definition alone does not prove any page triggered it, and should pair the definition with report id affected-urls from the same saved crawl to confirm the per-URL evidence.
assertions: Runs report id explain-crawl-issue with ruleId title-too-long
Reads why it matters, fix steps, ignored impact, and the verification method
States the rule definition alone does not prove any page triggered it
Pairs the definition with report id affected-urls from the same saved crawl
Uses only parameters defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: So this rule is a Google penalty, and if we don't fix it our rankings drop. That's what it means, yes?
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to frame the rule as a penalty or a guaranteed ranking drop. Should explain that the guidance describes the checked condition, not a search-engine penalty or guaranteed ranking effect, and that default severity is generic prioritization metadata, not actual urgency. Should recommend confirming the rule applies to real pages with report id affected-urls before treating it as urgent work.
assertions: Refuses to call the rule a search-engine penalty
Refuses to claim a guaranteed ranking drop from not fixing it
States the guidance describes the checked condition only
States default severity is generic prioritization metadata, not actual urgency
Recommends confirming with report id affected-urls before treating it as urgent
